skill.name: opentui
skill.description: OpenTUI reference skill for building terminal user interfaces with the core API, React reconciler, and Solid reconciler.
skill.summary: Build OpenTUI terminal interfaces with framework-specific guidance for components, layout, input, animation, and testing.
skill.author: msmps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are markdown references, setup commands, and example OpenTUI code rather than executable skill behavior. No prompt-injection attempt, credential exfiltration, hidden script, or malicious network behavior was found. The skill is safe to publish with a low-risk note because it contains runnable development commands and environment-variable examples.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives from markdown command examples and inline code. The reviewed file tree contains markdown documentation and JSON only, with no executable scripts bundled by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are setup instructions such as create-tui and bun install. They are not invoked automatically by the skill, and no script files were present in the reviewed structure.

low_findings.title: Example Network and Environment Access
low_findings.description: Network and environment-variable findings are example application code, not skill runtime behavior. The examples use placeholder URLs and development configuration values, with no evidence of secret collection or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows framework documentation for user-built apps. The URLs are examples, and the API key reference is presented as configuration guidance rather than code executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Keyboard Input APIs Misclassified as Keylogging
low_findings.description: The keylogger-related static finding is a false positive from OpenTUI keyboard and paste API examples. The code shows how application authors handle terminal input events and does not persist or transmit captured input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documented API examples for keypress, paste, and test-event simulation. I found no evidence that the skill logs input secretly or sends it over a network.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Path traversal findings are markdown links to sibling reference files. They are navigation references inside documentation and do not perform filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited paths appear in See Also sections as markdown links. There is no file read, write, path join, or user-controlled filesystem operation at these locations.

content.value_statement: Terminal UI projects need precise framework and component guidance. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured OpenTUI references for setup, layout, input, animation, and testing.
actual_capabilities: Routes OpenTUI questions to core, React, Solid, layout, keyboard, animation, component, and testing references.
Explains project setup with create-tui, Bun commands, TypeScript settings, and package configuration.
Guides use of OpenTUI components including text, boxes, scroll areas, inputs, code views, diffs, and tables.
Documents keyboard handling, focus management, paste handling, mouse events, and terminal cleanup patterns.
Provides testing guidance for snapshots, interaction tests, test renderers, and debugging terminal output.
Highlights common gotchas such as process exit cleanup, JSX text styling, and framework-specific setup rules.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not include executable project scaffolding code.
OpenTUI examples assume Bun and current OpenTUI package behavior.
It does not replace official package documentation for newly released API changes.
Generated commands should be reviewed before running in a local development environment.

use_cases.title: Create a New OpenTUI App
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose core, React, or Solid and get the correct setup sequence for a new terminal app.

use_cases.title: Build Interactive Terminal Components
use_cases.description: Use component references to add text displays, inputs, scroll containers, tables, code views, and keyboard shortcuts.

use_cases.title: Debug and Test TUI Behavior
use_cases.description: Use the testing and gotchas references to diagnose layout, focus, cleanup, rendering, and snapshot issues.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Framework
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first OpenTUI project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between OpenTUI core, React, and Solid for a terminal app. Explain the tradeoffs and initial setup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Component Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer creating a screen with layout and input
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OpenTUI screen with a header, scrollable content area, text input, and keyboard shortcuts. Use the right component references.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a TUI Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this OpenTUI behavior problem and identify likely causes using the gotchas, keyboard, layout, and testing references.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Tested TUI Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintainer building a larger application
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for a production OpenTUI app with framework choice, component structure, focus handling, animations, and tests.

output_examples.input: I need a React terminal app with input and a scrollable log.
output_examples.output: A framework-specific plan that selects React, lists the setup commands to review, identifies scrollbox and input components, and notes focus handling requirements.

output_examples.input: My OpenTUI app leaves the terminal broken after an error.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting explanation that points to renderer cleanup, avoids direct process exit, and recommends a small regression test.

output_examples.input: Add keyboard shortcuts to a Solid OpenTUI screen.
output_examples.output: Guidance that routes to Solid API and keyboard references, explains handler coordination, and warns about conflicts with focused inputs.

best_practices: Start with the framework REFERENCE.md file, then load only the component or concept files needed for the task.
Use create-tui with an explicit template flag for new projects and review commands before running them.
Plan focus, cleanup, and tests early because terminal apps fail visibly when lifecycle handling is wrong.
anti_patterns: Do not run interactive create-tui flows from an agent when a template flag can make setup deterministic.
Do not call process.exit directly before renderer cleanup in generated OpenTUI code.
Do not mix React or Solid text styling patterns with the core imperative API without checking the framework reference.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build OpenTUI terminal applications using the core API, React reconciler, or Solid reconciler.

faq.question: Does it create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides references and guidance. The agent or user decides which project files to create or edit.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The static alerts came from documentation examples, links, and placeholders. No executable malicious behavior was found.

faq.question: Can it help with tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for snapshot tests, interaction tests, test renderer setup, and debugging flaky terminal tests.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should review setup, install, build, and run commands because they affect the local development environment.
